title: Bootstrap muno repo: widget contract schemas + frontend skeleton + project home base (trip-planner Round 4a/6a)
description: Bootstrap `git@github.com:noetl/muno.git` (currently empty) as the trip-planner project's home base. Single Codex round that produces: (a) the canonical widget-contract JSON Schemas at `muno/playbooks/widget-contract/*.schema.json` (18 templates per the scoping doc), (b) the architecture doc explaining schema versioning and render-fallback rules, (c) the React 18 + TS + Vite + MUI frontend skeleton with schema-validating widget renderer infrastructure (stubs that render payload-as-JSON for now; Round 6b implements the real Material components), (d) all project meta â€” docs/architecture, docs/deployment, docs/auth, docs/tutorial, docs/runbooks skeletons; memory/ skeleton with claude/ + codex/ subdirs; .claude/ config; project-scoped scripts (memory_add.sh, memory_compact.sh, figma_fetch.sh mirror), AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, README.md, Dockerfile, nginx.conf. After the initial muno commit lands on its main, add `repos/muno` as a submodule under ai-meta. After this round, Rounds 4b (itinerary agent itself) and 6b (real widget Material rendering) run as independent parallel Codex rounds. Scoping reference: `sync/issues/2026-05-12-trip-planner-app-scoping.md` (sections 'muno is the home base', 'Pre-templated widgets', 'Initial muno repo shape').

pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.status: DONE â€” `noetl/muno` repo exists, empty, default branch not yet created. claude-cowork verified via `gh repo view noetl/muno`.
pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.what_codex_inherits: An empty GitHub repo at `git@github.com:noetl/muno.git`. No README, no default branch. The first push of this round creates `main` and seeds everything.
pre_handoff_for_kadyapam.no_new_secrets: Reuses existing `duffel-api-test`, `google-maps-widget-key`, `figma-access-token`. No new GCP setup.

design_brief_for_codex.round_split_context:
This is round 4a/6a â€” the combined widget-contract + bootstrap deliverable. After this lands:
  - Round 4b: implement `muno/playbooks/itinerary-planner.yaml` â€” the actual LLM-driven hybrid-input agent that consumes the schemas + mcp/firestore + mcp/duffel + mcp/google-places + mcp/amadeus.
  - Round 6b: implement the real Material widget components in `muno/src/components/widgets/*.tsx` replacing the JSON stubs.
Both 4b and 6b code against the contract this round freezes.
design_brief_for_codex.widget_catalogue_to_emit:
Eighteen templates per the scoping doc 'Pre-templated widgets' section. One JSON Schema file per template in `muno/playbooks/widget-contract/`:

  1. `bot_text.schema.json` â€” bot bubble. Fields: text (string, markdown allowed), timestamp (ISO).
  2. `user_text.schema.json` â€” user echo bubble. Fields: text, timestamp, user_avatar_url.
  3. `typing_indicator.schema.json` â€” `AdionaBot is typing...`. Fields: agent_name.
  4. `date_range_picker.schema.json` â€” interactive. Fields: min_date (ISO date), max_date, default_from?, default_to?, locale. Variant: `compact | full`. CTA: `submit`. Submitted value: { from, to, nights }.
  5. `party_picker.schema.json` â€” interactive. Fields: rooms_max, adults_max, children_max, allow_child_ages (bool). Submitted value: { rooms, adults, children: [{age}] }.
  6. `place_autocomplete_input.schema.json` â€” interactive. Fields: placeholder, suggestions (array of `{label, id, kind: airport|city|landmark}`), submit_on_select (bool). Submitted value: { label, id, kind }.
  7. `flight_list.schema.json` â€” list of flight_card payloads. Fields: title, items (array of flight_card payload), emphasis_offer_id?, total_count, currency. Variants: `default`.
  8. `flight_card.schema.json` â€” single offer. Fields: offer_id, price {total, currency}, itineraries[], carriers[], duration, stops, validating_airline. Variants: `compact | full | in_popover`. CTAs: `view_details | book_this`. ai_adjustments: `emphasis` (highlights the card), `annotations[]`.
  9. `hotel_list.schema.json` â€” list of hotel_card payloads. Fields: title, items, total_count, filter_summary, upsell_banner? {text, action_label, action_id}. Variants: `default`.
  10. `hotel_card.schema.json` â€” single property. Fields: hotel_id, name, location, star_rating, score, score_count, thumb_up_count, thumb_down_count, photos[] (urls), amenities[] (icon ids), price_per_night, currency, rooms_matching, available_rooms?, address, distance_from_center?. Variants: `compact | full | in_popover | map_marker`. CTAs: `watch_in_detail | show_numbers`. ai_adjustments: `emphasis`, `annotations[]`, `upsell_banner` (optional override of list-level banner).
  11. `hotel_compare.schema.json` â€” two hotel_card payloads side-by-side. Fields: items (array, length 2), comparison_facets[] (field names to highlight). Variant: `side_by_side`. (Optional in v1 â€” Kadyapam-locked deferral check; emit the schema anyway so it exists when needed.)
  12. `place_list.schema.json` â€” list of place_card payloads. Fields: title, items.
  13. `place_card.schema.json` â€” single place. Fields: place_id, name, types[], photos[], rating, rating_count, opening_hours?, address. Variants: `compact | full`. CTAs: `view_details | add_to_itinerary`.
  14. `action_chooser.schema.json` â€” illustrated CTA row. Fields: prompt_text, options (array of `{label, action_id, illustration_url?}`). Submitted value: { action_id }.
  15. `map_view.schema.json` â€” full-bleed map. Fields: center {lat, lng}, zoom, markers[] (array of `{lat, lng, label, hotel_id?, state: normal|selected|viewed}`), selected_marker_id?, filter_state {star_rating[], budget_min, budget_max, guest_rating_min}.
  16. `filter_panel.schema.json` â€” right-pane interactive. Fields: hotel_category_options[], budget_range {min, max, currency}, guest_rating_options[], applied {hotel_category[], budget_min, budget_max, guest_rating_min}. CTA: `clear_all | apply`.
  17. `property_block.schema.json` â€” right-pane slot accumulator. Fields: slots (ordered array of `{label, value, edit_action_id, missing: bool}`). Each slot becomes a row with an edit pencil if `edit_action_id` is set.
  18. `itinerary_summary.schema.json` â€” end-of-flow review. Fields: destination, dates, traveller_party, picked_flight (flight_card payload), picked_hotel (hotel_card payload), total_cost?, notes?. CTA: `confirm | edit`.

Also include these utility templates (5 more â€” call them v1 essentials):
  19. `order_confirmation.schema.json` â€” Duffel test order receipt. Fields: order_id, booking_reference, total_amount, total_currency, passengers[], slices[]. CTA: `view_full | new_search`.
  20. `notification.schema.json` â€” toast (`mes_notification` Figma component). Fields: kind (info|warning|error|success), text, dismissible.
  21. `error_card.schema.json` â€” graceful failure surface. Fields: title, description, retry_action_id?, contact_support? (bool).
  22. `clarify_question.schema.json` â€” bot asks back when ambiguous. Fields: question_text, options (array of `{label, action_id}`).
  23. `loading_card.schema.json` â€” placeholder while async tool runs. Fields: tool_name, started_at, expected_duration_seconds?.

Total: 23 schemas. (The scoping doc said 18 â€” adding 5 utility ones â€” clarify, loading, error, notification, order_confirmation â€” keeps the agent's life easier with no v1 cost.)
design_brief_for_codex.widget_contract_doc:
`muno/docs/architecture/widget-contract.md` â€” explains:
  - The envelope shape.
  - The closed-catalogue principle (AI emits one of the 23; unknown types fall back to bot_text).
  - Schema versioning: bump `schema_version` when a breaking change to a template's payload schema lands; the renderer keeps both N and N-1 implementations during the migration window.
  - The ai_adjustments contract: AI can adjust within the schema's declared knobs (variant pick, emphasis, conditional banners, annotations) but cannot invent new fields.
  - The render-fallback rule: if the renderer can't validate or render, surface a `bot_text` widget with the AI's natural-language description (the agent's LLM step always produces a description alongside the widget, so the fallback is graceful).
  - The build-time TS type generation: `npm run build` runs `json-schema-to-typescript` over `playbooks/widget-contract/*.schema.json` and emits `src/contracts/widgets.ts`. The agent (Round 4b) consumes the JSON schemas; the frontend (Round 6b) consumes the TS types.
design_brief_for_codex.muno_repo_structure:
Implement the full structure documented in the scoping doc's 'Initial muno repo shape' section, with these specific stack choices: - React: 18.x latest - TypeScript: 5.5+ - Vite: 7.x (match repos/gui) - MUI: 6.x (Material UI v6+ for Material Design 3) - React Router: 6.x - Axios for NoETL server API calls - ajv (JSON Schema validation at render boundary) - json-schema-to-typescript (devDep, runs in `npm run build`) - react-i18next for the `EN` / `RU` toggle - Firebase JS SDK for live Firestore listening (frontend reads only; writes go through NoETL mcp/firestore) - Node target: 20 LTS Key files to create with non-trivial content (not just stubs): - `package.json` with all deps + scripts (dev, build, type-check, test, lint). - `vite.config.ts` mirroring repos/gui's pattern with MUI + i18n setup. - `tsconfig.json` and `tsconfig.node.json`. - `src/main.tsx` â€” React entry with MUI ThemeProvider and i18n init. - `src/App.tsx` â€” three-column shell skeleton (Sidebar, ChatThread, RightPane). - `src/theme.ts` â€” placeholder MUI theme with Adiona blue (#1976D2 placeholder; Round 6b feeds real Figma tokens once `file_variables:read` scope is available). - `src/locales/en.json` and `src/locales/ru.json` â€” placeholders with greeting strings. - `src/api/noetlClient.ts` â€” axios wrapper with base URL from env. - `src/api/firestoreClient.ts` â€” Firebase JS SDK init + live listener helper. - `src/contracts/widgets.ts` â€” GENERATED FILE (committed) from the build step. - `src/components/widgets/*.tsx` â€” 23 stub components, one per template. Each stub takes the validated payload and renders `
{JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2)}
` inside an MUI Card. Round 6b replaces these. - `src/components/WidgetRenderer.tsx` â€” central renderer that takes a wire envelope, validates against ajv + the schemas, dispatches to the right stub or falls back to `bot_text`. - `Dockerfile` mirroring repos/gui's pattern (multi-stage: node builder â†’ nginx serve). - `nginx.conf` mirroring repos/gui. Meta-files: - `README.md` â€” one-pager: what muno is, how to run dev, link to scoping doc. - `AGENTS.md` â€” shared AI entry point (read this + memory + scoping). - `CLAUDE.md` â€” Claude Code entry point (mirror ai-meta's pattern, scoped to muno). - `.gitignore` â€” node_modules, dist, .env, etc. - `.claude/settings.json` â€” permission allowlist for npm/vite/tsc/vitest. Use ai-meta's settings.json as the template. - `.claude/rules/` â€” empty for now; v2 can symlink shared rules from ai-meta. - `.claude/skills/` â€” empty for now. Docs skeleton (one stub README per subdirectory + ARCHITECTURE.md at root of docs): - `docs/architecture/event-sourcing.md` â€” copy the relevant section from the scoping doc. - `docs/architecture/widget-contract.md` â€” the doc described above (full content this round). - `docs/architecture/agent-design.md` â€” stub (Round 4b fleshes out). - `docs/architecture/data-model.md` â€” copy the Firestore data model from the scoping doc. - `docs/deployment/kind-setup.md` â€” stub. - `docs/deployment/gke-setup.md` â€” stub. - `docs/deployment/figma-pat-setup.md` â€” copy the recipe from ai-meta scoping. - `docs/auth/firebase-setup.md` â€” stub (later). - `docs/auth/guest-mode.md` â€” stub. - `docs/tutorial/end-to-end.md` â€” stub (Round 7 cap-stone). - `docs/runbooks/replay-a-session.md` â€” pointer to ai-meta/scripts/firestore_replay.sh and Round 3 memory entry. Memory skeleton: - `memory/current.md` â€” bootstrapped with 'Trip-planner muno project initialized 2026-05-13'. - `memory/inbox/claude/2026/05/.gitkeep` - `memory/inbox/codex/2026/05/.gitkeep` - `memory/compact/.gitkeep` Scripts: - `scripts/memory_add.sh` â€” adapted from ai-meta/scripts/memory_add.sh, writes to muno/memory/inbox/claude/ by default (subdir selectable via --kind=claude|codex flag). - `scripts/memory_compact.sh` â€” adapted similarly. - `scripts/figma_fetch.sh` â€” exact copy of ai-meta's helper (uses the same `figma-access-token` GCP secret). - `scripts/build_widget_contracts.sh` â€” invokes json-schema-to-typescript to regenerate src/contracts/widgets.ts.
design_brief_for_codex.no_real_widget_rendering: The stub components in `src/components/widgets/*.tsx` render `<pre>` JSON. This is INTENTIONAL â€” Round 6b's job is the real Material rendering. The schema validation + dispatch infrastructure is real and works; the visual layer is the stub. This unblocks Round 4b (agent emits real wire envelopes, renderer validates and routes, stubs prove the contract end-to-end).
design_brief_for_codex.submodule_wiring_in_ai_meta:
After muno's initial commit lands on its main branch:
  ```bash
  cd /Volumes/X10/projects/noetl/ai-meta
  git submodule add git@github.com:noetl/muno.git repos/muno
  git commit -m 'chore(sync): add repos/muno submodule for trip-planner project home base'
  ```
The submodule add is part of this round's close-out â€” the result JSON should record the ai-meta commit SHA that adds the submodule. ai-meta is NOT pushed by Codex per the standing rule; Kadyapam handles the ai-meta push after reviewing.

1_verify_pre_handoff.objective: Confirm muno repo is empty and the codex environment can push to it.
spec: `gh repo view noetl/muno --json name,defaultBranchRef,isEmpty -q '.isEmpty'` â†’ must be `true`.
Confirm Codex has push access to `git@github.com:noetl/muno.git` (will be exercised in phase 5).
No GCP pre-flight needed â€” bootstrapping is pure code; live API calls happen only in later rounds.
If repo is non-empty: AMBER + STOP (don't overwrite existing content).
1_verify_pre_handoff.verify: muno is empty; push access exists.

2_author_widget_contract.objective: Produce all 23 widget schemas + the envelope schema + the architecture doc.
spec: Author `muno/playbooks/widget-contract/_envelope.schema.json` (JSON Schema draft-07) describing the common wrapper with `widget_type`, `variant`, `payload`, `ai_adjustments`, `schema_version`.

Author the 23 individual template schemas per `design_brief_for_codex.widget_catalogue_to_emit`. Each schema: draft-07, `$id` matching its file path, descriptive `title` and `description`, explicit `required` arrays, `additionalProperties: false` to keep the surface tight.

Author `muno/docs/architecture/widget-contract.md` per `design_brief_for_codex.widget_contract_doc` â€” covers envelope, closed-catalogue principle, schema versioning, ai_adjustments contract, render-fallback rule, build-time TS type generation.
2_author_widget_contract.verify: All 24 schema files validate as valid JSON Schema draft-07 (use `ajv compile` to check). widget-contract.md renders cleanly as markdown.

3_author_muno_frontend_skeleton.objective: Build the React 18 + TS + Vite + MUI scaffold + schema-validating widget renderer with JSON stubs.
spec: Run `npm install` (or `pnpm install`) clean against `package.json` to confirm deps resolve.
`npm run type-check` passes against the skeleton.
`npm run build` succeeds and emits `dist/`. The build step also regenerates `src/contracts/widgets.ts` from the schemas.

Implement `src/components/WidgetRenderer.tsx`: takes an envelope, validates against the 23 schemas via ajv, dispatches to the matching stub OR falls back to a bot_text widget describing the validation failure.

Each of the 23 stub components is roughly 20-30 lines: MUI Card wrapper + `
{JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2)}
` + a TODO comment referencing Round 6b.
Three-column App shell with Sidebar, ChatThread, RightPane stubs. ChatThread uses WidgetRenderer to render an array of envelopes.

i18n init: `useTranslation` available, en/ru locale files seeded with placeholder strings (`app_name`, `sidebar.guest`, `sidebar.orders`, `sidebar.searches`, `chat.start_search_again`, `chat.send`, etc.).
Dockerfile + nginx.conf mirroring repos/gui's pattern. `docker build .` succeeds locally (verify in Codex sandbox).
3_author_muno_frontend_skeleton.verify: npm install, type-check, build, and docker build all succeed. The schema validator dispatches to all 23 stubs correctly given a sample envelope per template.

4_author_meta_files_and_skeletons.objective: Lay down docs, memory, .claude, scripts, and project meta files.
spec: Author README, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .gitignore at repo root.

Author all `docs/{architecture,deployment,auth,tutorial,runbooks}/*.md` files per the design brief â€” full content for widget-contract.md, event-sourcing.md, data-model.md, figma-pat-setup.md (copied from existing sources); stubs for the rest with a header + 'TBD by Round X' note.
Author memory skeleton â€” `memory/current.md` bootstrapped with init note, `memory/inbox/{claude,codex}/2026/05/.gitkeep`, `memory/compact/.gitkeep`.
Author `.claude/settings.json` with permission allowlists (npm, vite, tsc, vitest, docker) modelled on ai-meta's settings. Empty `.claude/rules/` and `.claude/skills/` dirs with .gitkeep.

Author scripts: `memory_add.sh`, `memory_compact.sh` (adapted from ai-meta with `--kind=claude|codex` flag), `figma_fetch.sh` (exact copy from ai-meta), `build_widget_contracts.sh` (invokes json-schema-to-typescript).
4_author_meta_files_and_skeletons.verify: Tree structure matches the scoping doc's 'Initial muno repo shape' section. All scripts are executable.

5_first_commit_and_push_to_noetl_muno.objective: Push the initial commit to the empty `noetl/muno` repo, creating `main`.
spec: From within the muno workspace: `git init`, `git add .`, then `git commit -m 'chore: bootstrap muno (trip-planner chat app + widget contract + playbooks + docs + memory + .claude)'`.
`git branch -M main`.
`git remote add origin git@github.com:noetl/muno.git`.
`git push -u origin main`.
Open NO pull request â€” this is the initial bootstrap commit, lands directly on main per Kadyapam's stated workflow (`git push -u origin main` from the empty-repo instructions in the scoping doc).
Capture the merge SHA for the result JSON.
5_first_commit_and_push_to_noetl_muno.verify: noetl/muno main HEAD points at the bootstrap commit; `gh repo view noetl/muno --json defaultBranchRef -q '.defaultBranchRef.name'` returns `main`.

6_add_submodule_to_ai_meta.objective: Register `repos/muno` as a submodule under ai-meta and stage the change for Kadyapam to push.
spec: `cd ai-meta && git submodule add git@github.com:noetl/muno.git repos/muno`.
Commit the submodule add: `git commit -m 'chore(sync): add repos/muno submodule for trip-planner project home base (Round 4a/6a)'`.
Do NOT push ai-meta â€” leave the commit local per the standing rule.
Record the ai-meta commit SHA in the result JSON.
6_add_submodule_to_ai_meta.verify: ai-meta has a clean commit adding repos/muno as a submodule; submodule status shows the muno tip SHA matching phase 5.

7_close_out.objective: Result JSON, codex-spike validation log, memory entry, scoping-doc update.
spec: Result file: `bridge/outbox/20260513-000000-muno-bootstrap-widget-contract.result.json`. Mirrors prior round result shapes. Required fields: id, status, summary, muno_initial_commit_sha, ai_meta_submodule_add_sha, schemas_count (24 = 1 envelope + 23 templates), build_smoke {npm_install, type_check, vite_build, docker_build}, widget_renderer_dispatch_smoke, cleanup {no_secrets_committed}, verdict.
Append a paragraph to `bridge/outbox/codex-spike-green-validation.md`.

Outcome memory: this lands at the boundary where muno exists. Per the scoping doc rule, Codex outcome memory for trip-planner work goes to `muno/memory/inbox/codex/2026/05/<timestamp>-muno-bootstrap-widget-contract-green.md`. Since muno is just being created in this round, this entry is part of the bootstrap commit (i.e., it lands in muno itself). Place a cross-link entry at `ai-meta/memory/inbox/2026/05/<timestamp>-muno-bootstrap-widget-contract-green.md` that points at the muno-side entry.
Update `ai-meta/sync/issues/2026-05-12-trip-planner-app-scoping.md` adding a 'Round 4a/6a GREEN' note under the Round 6 section.
7_close_out.verify: Result JSON written, validation log appended, memory cross-link in place, scoping doc updated.

architectural_rules: Single Codex round. Do NOT split into multiple smaller rounds.
muno repo initial push lands directly on `main`. No PR. The repo is empty; this IS the initial commit.
All 23 widget templates + envelope schema land in this round. No 'TBD' template schemas.
Widget components in `src/components/widgets/*.tsx` are STUBS rendering `<pre>` JSON. Round 6b replaces them. Do NOT invent a real Material rendering in this round.
Build pipeline must produce `src/contracts/widgets.ts` from the schemas at `npm run build` time. The generated file IS committed (for IDE go-to-definition support).
i18n is wired but only with placeholder strings.
Theme uses placeholder Material colors. Real Figma-variable-driven theme comes in Round 6b once `file_variables:read` scope is added to the Figma PAT.
Schema validation library: ajv. Schema â†’ TS types: json-schema-to-typescript. Both in package.json.
ai-meta submodule add is committed locally but NOT pushed by Codex. Kadyapam pushes ai-meta.
No new GCP secrets in this round.
Never commit secrets, .env files, or node_modules. .gitignore covers them.
If muno repo is not empty: AMBER + STOP (don't overwrite).
result_file_instructions: Write `bridge/outbox/20260513-000000-muno-bootstrap-widget-contract.result.json`. Top-level: {id, status, summary, muno_repo_url, muno_initial_commit_sha, ai_meta_submodule_add_sha, schemas_count, frontend_smokes: {npm_install_ok, type_check_ok, vite_build_ok, docker_build_ok, widget_renderer_dispatch_ok}, file_count_breakdown: {schemas, src_components, docs, scripts, configs, total}, cleanup, verdict}.
references: sync/issues/2026-05-12-trip-planner-app-scoping.md â€” full trip-planner scoping; sections 'muno is the home base', 'Pre-templated widgets', 'Initial muno repo shape', 'Memory entries â€” where each one lives'
memory/inbox/2026/05/20260512-235900-firestore-mcp-event-sourcing-green.md â€” Round 3 outcome; the storage substrate the agent will use
memory/inbox/2026/05/20260512-230000-duffel-stays-unavailable-round-2.md â€” Round 4 hotels source locked to Amadeus
repos/gui/package.json â€” reference toolchain for React + TS + Vite versions
repos/gui/Dockerfile + nginx.conf â€” packaging pattern to mirror
https://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema â€” JSON Schema draft-07 spec
https://github.com/bcherny/json-schema-to-typescript â€” schema â†’ TS types generator
